category_title: Young child with severe autism exhibiting physical aggression to escape demands or gain attention
minimal_context: Lauren is sitting at a table in her classroom for one-on-one instruction to label picture cards immediately following morning circle time.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Lauren, it is time to label the picture cards. What is on this card?

STUDENT: No.

TUTOR: Look at the card. Tell me what this is.

STUDENT: (Pushes the picture card away) Done.

TUTOR: We need to finish these cards. Look here.

STUDENT: (Screams loudly, kicks the table, and grabs the teacher's arm) No!

options.A: Point alternately to specific picture cards on the table, directing the student to label each one.
options.B: Immediately remove all demands and picture cards from the table for 60 seconds.
options.C: Respond by saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
options.D: Use a request for clarification, asking the student for explanations and elaborations about the picture cards.
